main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct mode_descriptor_t
Metadata for a mode.

Macros


#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'
#define VERSION ASCII_CHAT_VERSION_FULL
#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Typedefs


typedef int(* mode_entry_point_t) (void)

Functions


atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO (g_should_exit)
bool should_exit (void)
void signal_exit (void)
void set_interrupt_callback (void(*cb)(void))
void setup_signal_handlers (void)
int main (int argc, char *argv[])

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Unified Binary - Mode Dispatcher and Entry Point

This file implements the main entry point for the unified ascii-chat binary, which provides server, client, mirror, and discovery service functionality in a single executable.

The dispatcher now delegates ALL option parsing (including mode detection and binary-level options) to options_init(), then simply dispatches to the appropriate mode-specific entry point based on the detected mode.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

3.0

Fix: CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT properly configured for macOS builds WebRTC: Pass CMAKE_OSX_SYSROOT to subprocess for Threads discovery Test: Running macOS builds with WebRTC Threads fix

Definition in file main.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define APP_NAME 'ascii-chat'

#define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()

Value:

do {                                                                                                                 \


  static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \


  if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \


    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \


    _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \


  }                                                                                                                  \
} while (0)




Definition at line 190 of file main.c.


190 #define REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS()                                                                                      \
191   do {                                                                                                                 \
192     static bool _global_atomics_registered = false;                                                                    \
193     if (!_global_atomics_registered) {                                                                                 \
194       NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_exit, "application_exit_flag", NULL);                                            \
195       _global_atomics_registered = true;                                                                               \
196     }                                                                                                                  \
197   } while (0)

atomic_t g_should_exit ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO

Global flag indicating application should exit (used by all

References g_should_exit.






int
  main (int argc, char * argv[])


Definition at line 472 of file main.c.


472                                  {
473   // Set global argc/argv for early argv inspection (e.g., in terminal.c)
474   g_argc = argc;
475   g_argv = argv;
476 
477   // Validate basic argument structure
478   if (argc < 1 || argv == NULL || argv[0] == NULL) {
479     return 1;
480   }
481 
482   // Show cursor early in case a previous session crashed with it hidden
483   (void)terminal_cursor_show();
484 
485   // Initialize the named registry for debugging (allows --debug-state to show registered synchronization primitives)
486 #ifndef NDEBUG
487   named_init();
488   // Initialize atomic operations debug tracking
489   debug_atomic_init();
490   // Register global atomics with descriptive names for debug sync state monitoring
491   REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS();
492   // Static atomics are now auto-registered via ATOMIC_INIT_AUTO macro at definition time
493   // Register all packet types from the packet_type_t enum
494   named_registry_register_packet_types();
495 #endif
496 
497   // Register the main thread IMMEDIATELY after named_init() to ensure it's available for all subsequent allocations
498 #ifndef NDEBUG
499 #ifdef _WIN32
500   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD(GetCurrentThread(), "main", NULL);
501 #else
502   NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD((asciichat_thread_t)asciichat_thread_self(), "main", NULL);
503 #endif
504   // Also save main thread ID for memory reporting (must be very early)
505   debug_sync_set_main_thread_id();
506 #endif
507 
508   // VERY FIRST: Scan for --color BEFORE ANY logging initialization
509   // This sets global flags that persist through cleanup, enabling --color to force colors
510   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
511     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "--color=true") == 0) {
512       g_color_flag_passed = true;
513       g_color_flag_value = true;
514       break;
515     } else if (strncmp(argv[i], "--color=", 8) == 0) {
516       g_color_flag_passed = true;
517       g_color_flag_value = strcmp(argv[i], "--color=false") != 0;
518       break;
519     }
520   }
521 
522   // SECOND: Scan for --grep BEFORE ANY logging initialization
523   // This ensures ALL logs (including from shared_init) can be filtered
524   // Supports multiple --grep patterns (ORed together)
525   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
526     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--grep") == 0) {
527       const char *pattern = argv[i + 1];
528       asciichat_error_t filter_result = grep_init(pattern);
529       if (filter_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
530         log_error("Invalid --grep pattern or invalid flags: \"%s\" - use /pattern/flags format (e.g., \"/query/ig\" or \"/literal/F\")", pattern);
531         return 1;
532       }
533       i++; // Skip the pattern argument
534     }
535   }
536 
537   // Detect terminal capabilities early so colored help output works
538   // Logging will be initialized by asciichat_shared_init() before options_init()
539   log_redetect_terminal_capabilities();
540   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
541   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
542 
543   // Warn if Release build was built from dirty working tree
544 #if ASCII_CHAT_GIT_IS_DIRTY
545   if (strcmp(ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE, "Release") == 0) {
546     // Dirty git warning will be logged after logging initialization
547   }
548 #endif
549 
550   // Load color scheme early (from config files and CLI) before logging initialization
551   // This allows logging to use the correct colors from the start
552   options_colorscheme_init_early(argc, argv);
553 
554   // Parse --color-scheme from argv early to set logging colors for help output
555   const char *colorscheme_name = "pastel"; // default
556   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
557     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--color-scheme") == 0) {
558       colorscheme_name = argv[i + 1];
559       break;
560     }
561   }
562 
563   // Load and apply colorscheme to logging BEFORE options_init() so help gets colors
564   color_scheme_t scheme;
565   if (colorscheme_load_builtin(colorscheme_name, &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
566     log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
567   } else {
568     // Fall back to default pastel if scheme not found
569     if (colorscheme_load_builtin("pastel", &scheme) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
570       log_set_color_scheme(&scheme);
571     }
572   }
573 
574   // Initialize logging colors so they're ready for help output
575   log_init_colors();
576 
577   // EARLY PARSE: Find the mode position (first positional argument)
578   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
579   int mode_position = -1;
580   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
581     if (argv[i][0] != '-') {
582       // Found a positional argument - this is the mode or session string
583       mode_position = i;
584       break;
585     }
586 
587     // Skip option and its argument if needed
588     // Check if this is an option that takes a required argument
589     const char *arg = argv[i];
590     const char *opt_name = arg;
591     if (arg[0] == '-') {
592       opt_name = arg + (arg[1] == '-' ? 2 : 1);
593     }
594 
595     // Handle --option=value format
596     if (strchr(opt_name, '=')) {
597       continue; // Value is part of this arg, no need to skip next
598     }
599 
600     // Options that take required arguments
601     if (strcmp(arg, "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "-L") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-level") == 0 ||
602         strcmp(arg, "--config") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--color-scheme") == 0 || strcmp(arg, "--log-template") == 0) {
603       if (i + 1 < argc) {
604         i++; // Skip the argument value
605       }
606     }
607   }
608 
609   // EARLY PARSE: Determine mode from argv to know if this is client-like mode
610   // The first positional argument (after options) is the mode: client, server, mirror, discovery, acds
611   bool is_client_like_mode = false;
612   if (mode_position > 0) {
613     const char *first_arg = argv[mode_position];
614     if (strcmp(first_arg, "client") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "mirror") == 0 || strcmp(first_arg, "discovery") == 0) {
615       is_client_like_mode = true;
616     }
617   }
618 
619   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log file from argv (--log-file or -L)
620   // Must appear BEFORE the mode
621   // Generate default log path based on build type and mode
622   char default_log_path[PATH_MAX];
623   asciichat_mode_t detected_mode = MODE_INVALID;
624   if (mode_position > 0) {
625     const char *mode_str = argv[mode_position];
626     // Convert mode string to enum
627     if (strcmp(mode_str, "server") == 0) {
628       detected_mode = MODE_SERVER;
629     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "client") == 0) {
630       detected_mode = MODE_CLIENT;
631     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "mirror") == 0) {
632       detected_mode = MODE_MIRROR;
633     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "acds") == 0 || strcmp(mode_str, "discovery-service") == 0) {
634       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE;
635     } else if (strcmp(mode_str, "discovery") == 0) {
636       detected_mode = MODE_DISCOVERY;
637     }
638   }
639   generate_default_log_path(detected_mode, default_log_path, sizeof(default_log_path));
640 
641   const char *log_file = default_log_path;
642   int max_search = (mode_position > 0) ? mode_position : argc;
643   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
644     if ((strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0)) {
645       log_file = argv[i + 1];
646       break;
647     }
648   }
649 
650   // EARLY PARSE: Check for --json (JSON logging format)
651   // If JSON format is enabled, we'll use the JSON filename during early init
652   // --json MUST appear BEFORE the mode
653   bool early_json_format = false;
654   if (mode_position > 0) {
655     for (int i = 1; i < mode_position; i++) {
656       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
657         early_json_format = true;
658         break;
659       }
660     }
661   } else {
662     // If no mode found, search entire argv
663     for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
664       if (strcmp(argv[i], "--json") == 0) {
665         early_json_format = true;
666         break;
667       }
668     }
669   }
670 
671   // EARLY PARSE: Extract log template from argv (--log-template)
672   // Note: This is for format templates, different from --log-format which is the output format
673   // Binary-level options must appear BEFORE the mode
674   const char *early_log_template = NULL;
675   bool early_log_template_console_only = false;
676   for (int i = 1; i < max_search - 1; i++) {
677     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-template") == 0) {
678       early_log_template = argv[i + 1];
679       break;
680     }
681   }
682   for (int i = 1; i < max_search; i++) {
683     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-format-console-only") == 0) {
684       early_log_template_console_only = true;
685       break;
686     }
687   }
688 
689   // Initialize shared subsystems BEFORE options_init()
690   // This ensures options parsing can use properly configured logging with colors
691   // If JSON format is requested, don't write text logs to file
692   // All logs will be JSON formatted later once options_init() runs
693   const char *early_log_file = early_json_format ? NULL : log_file;
694   asciichat_error_t init_result = asciichat_shared_init(early_log_file, is_client_like_mode);
695   if (init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
696     return init_result;
697   }
698 
699   // Route logs to stderr if stdout is piped (MUST happen early, before options_init logs)
700   // This keeps stdout clean for data output (e.g., --snapshot mode piped to file)
701   if (terminal_should_force_stderr()) {
702     log_set_force_stderr(true);
703   }
704 
705   // Register cleanup of shared subsystems to run on normal exit
706   // Library code doesn't call atexit() - the application is responsible
707   // IMPORTANT: atexit handlers run in LIFO order - last registered runs first
708   // So register order (top to bottom) = execution order (bottom to top):
709   // 1. PCRE2 cleanup (runs last) - frees compiled regexes after all code stops using them
710   // 2. Path cleanup (runs 3rd) - now a no-op but kept for API compatibility
711   // 3. Shared destroy (runs 2nd) - frees all shared resources
712   // 4. Cursor show (runs 1st) - shows cursor before exit
713   (void)atexit(asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all);
714   (void)atexit(path_cleanup_thread_locals);
715   (void)atexit(asciichat_shared_destroy);
716   (void)atexit(on_exit_show_cursor);
717 
718   // SECRET: Check for --backtrace (debug builds only) BEFORE options_init()
719   // Prints a backtrace and exits immediately - useful for debugging hangs
720 #ifndef NDEBUG
721   for (int i = 1; i < argc; i++) {
722     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--backtrace") == 0) {
723       log_info("=== Backtrace at startup ===");
724       backtrace_print_simple(0);
725       log_info("=== End Backtrace ===");
726       asciichat_shared_destroy();
727       return 0;
728     }
729   }
730 #endif
731 
732   // NOW parse all options - can use logging with colors!
733   asciichat_error_t options_result = options_init(argc, argv);
734   if (options_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
735     asciichat_error_context_t error_ctx;
736     if (HAS_ERRNO(&error_ctx)) {
737       log_error("Error: %s", error_ctx.context_message);
738     } else {
739       log_error("Error: Failed to initialize options (error code: %d)", options_result);
740     }
741 
742     // Clean up options state before exiting
743     options_state_destroy();
744     options_cleanup_schema();
745 
746     exit(options_result);
747   }
748 
749   // Get parsed options
750   const options_t *opts = options_get();
751   if (!opts) {
752     log_error("Error: Options not initialized");
753     return 1;
754   }
755 
756   // Determine final log file path (use mode-specific default from options if available)
757   // Determine output format early to decide on filename and logging strategy
758   bool use_json_logging = GET_OPTION(json);
759 
760   // Determine the log filename
761   // Check if user explicitly passed --log-file (not just the mode-specific default from options_init)
762   bool user_specified_log_file = false;
763   for (int i = 1; i < argc - 1; i++) {
764     if (strcmp(argv[i], "--log-file") == 0 || strcmp(argv[i], "-L") == 0) {
765       user_specified_log_file = true;
766       break;
767     }
768   }
769 
770   const char *final_log_file = opts->log_file;
771   char json_filename_buf[256];
772 
773   if (use_json_logging) {
774     // When JSON logging is enabled, determine JSON output filename
775     if (user_specified_log_file) {
776       // User explicitly specified a log file - use it exactly for JSON output
777       SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
778     } else {
779       // Using default: replace .log with .json in the mode-specific default
780       // e.g., server.log -> server.json, mirror.log -> mirror.json, etc.
781       size_t len = strlen(final_log_file);
782       if (len > 4 && strcmp(&final_log_file[len - 4], ".log") == 0) {
783         // File ends with .log - replace with .json
784         SAFE_STRNCPY(json_filename_buf, final_log_file, sizeof(json_filename_buf) - 1);
785         // Replace .log with .json
786         asciichat_error_t strcpy_result = SAFE_STRCPY(&json_filename_buf[len - 4],
787                                                        sizeof(json_filename_buf) - (len - 4), ".json");
788         if (strcpy_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
789           log_error("Failed to write .json extension: %s", asciichat_error_string(strcpy_result));
790         }
791       } else {
792         // File doesn't end with .log - just append .json
793         snprintf(json_filename_buf, sizeof(json_filename_buf), "%s.json", final_log_file);
794       }
795     }
796     final_log_file = json_filename_buf;
797     // For JSON mode: Initialize logging without file (text will be disabled)
798     // We'll set up JSON output separately below
799     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
800   } else {
801     // Text logging mode: use the file from options (which is mode-specific default or user-specified)
802     // Note: log_init() was already called in asciichat_shared_init()
803   }
804 
805   // Apply custom log template if specified (use early parsed value if available, otherwise use options)
806   const char *final_format = early_log_template ? early_log_template : GET_OPTION(log_template);
807   bool final_format_console_only =
808       early_log_template ? early_log_template_console_only : GET_OPTION(log_format_console_only);
809   if (final_format && final_format[0] != '\0') {
810     asciichat_error_t fmt_result = log_set_format(final_format, final_format_console_only);
811     if (fmt_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
812       log_error("Failed to apply custom log format");
813     }
814   }
815 
816   // Configure JSON output if requested
817   if (use_json_logging) {
818     // Open the JSON file for output
819     int json_fd = platform_open("main_log_file", final_log_file, O_CREAT | O_RDWR | O_TRUNC, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE);
820     if (json_fd >= 0) {
821       log_set_json_output(json_fd);
822     } else {
823       // Failed to open JSON file - report error and exit
824       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open JSON output file: %s", final_log_file);
825       return ERROR_CONFIG;
826     }
827   }
828 
829   // Initialize colors now that logging is fully initialized
830   // This must happen after log_init() since log_init_colors() checks if g_log.initialized
831   log_init_colors();
832 
833   // Apply quiet mode - disables terminal output
834   // Status screen mode only disables terminal output if terminal is interactive
835   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
836   if (GET_OPTION(quiet) ||
837       (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && GET_OPTION(status_screen) && terminal_is_interactive())) {
838     log_set_terminal_output(false);
839   }
840 
841   // Initialize palette based on command line options
842   const char *custom_chars = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : NULL;
843   if (apply_palette_config(GET_OPTION(palette_type), custom_chars) != 0) {
844     FATAL(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to apply palette configuration");
845   }
846 
847   // Set quiet mode for memory debugging
848 #if defined(DEBUG_MEMORY) && !defined(USE_MIMALLOC_DEBUG) && !defined(NDEBUG)
849   debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(GET_OPTION(quiet));
850 #endif
851 
852   // Truncate log if it's already too large
853   log_truncate_if_large();
854   fflush(stderr);
855 
856   // Handle --help and --version (these are detected and flagged by options_init)
857   // Terminal capabilities already initialized before options_init() at startup
858   if (opts->help) {
859     print_usage(opts->detected_mode);
860     fflush(NULL);
861     _Exit(0);
862   }
863 
864   if (opts->version) {
865     log_set_terminal_output(true);
866     action_show_version();
867     // action_show_version() calls _Exit(), so we don't reach here
868   }
869 
870 #ifndef NDEBUG
871   // Handle --debug-state (debug builds only)
872   // Sleep for specified time AFTER mode initialization so locks are created
873   if (IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT(debug_sync_state_time, opts) && opts->debug_sync_state_time > 0.0) {
874     log_info("Will print sync state after %f seconds", opts->debug_sync_state_time);
875   }
876 #endif
877 
878   // For server mode with status screen: disable terminal output only if interactive
879   // In non-interactive mode (piped output), logs go to stdout/stderr normally
880   // The status screen (when shown) will capture and display logs in its buffer instead
881   if (opts->detected_mode == MODE_SERVER && opts->status_screen && terminal_is_interactive()) {
882     log_set_terminal_output(false);
883   }
884 
885   log_dev("Logging initialized to %s", final_log_file);
886 
887   // Note: We do NOT auto-disable colors when stdout appears to be piped, because:
888   // 1. Tools like ripgrep can display ANSI colors when piped
889   // 2. Sandboxed/containerized environments may report false positives for isatty()
890   // 3. Users can explicitly disable colors with --color=false if needed
891   // Color behavior is now fully controlled by --color and --color-mode options.
892 
893 #ifndef NDEBUG
894   // Initialize lock debugging system after logging is fully set up
895   log_debug("Initializing lock debug system...");
896   int debug_sync_result = debug_sync_init();
897   if (debug_sync_result != 0) {
898     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync system initialization failed");
899     FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Debug sync system initialization failed");
900   }
901   log_debug("Debug sync system initialized successfully");
902 
903   // Initialize memory debug system
904   // TEMPORARILY DISABLED: these calls hang in non-interactive mode
905   // log_debug("Initializing memory debug system...");
906   // int debug_memory_result = debug_memory_thread_init();
907   // if (debug_memory_result != 0) {
908   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug system initialization failed");
909   //   FATAL(ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, "Memory debug system initialization failed");
910   // }
911   // log_debug("Memory debug system initialized successfully");
912 
913   // Start memory debug (now just prints report directly)
914   // if (debug_memory_thread_start() != 0) {
915   //   LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Memory debug startup failed");
916   //   FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Memory debug startup failed");
917   // }
918 
919 #ifndef _WIN32
920   // Unblock SIGUSR1 and SIGUSR2 at process level to ensure delivery
921   sigset_t set;
922   sigemptyset(&set);
923   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR1);
924   sigaddset(&set, SIGUSR2);
925   sigprocmask(SIG_UNBLOCK, &set, NULL);
926 
927   // Register SIGUSR1 to trigger synchronization debugging output in all modes
928   signal_handler_t old_usr1 = platform_signal(SIGUSR1, common_handle_sigusr1);
929   if (old_usr1 == SIG_ERR) {
930     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR1 handler");
931   } else {
932     log_debug("SIGUSR1 handler registered successfully");
933   }
934 
935   // Register SIGUSR2 to trigger memory report in all modes
936   signal_handler_t old_usr2 = platform_signal(SIGUSR2, common_handle_sigusr2);
937   if (old_usr2 == SIG_ERR) {
938     log_warn("Failed to register SIGUSR2 handler");
939   } else {
940     log_debug("SIGUSR2 handler registered successfully");
941   }
942 #endif
943 #endif
944 
945   if (opts->fps > 0) {
946     if (opts->fps < 1 || opts->fps > 144) {
947       log_warn("FPS value %d out of range (1-144), using default", opts->fps);
948     } else {
949       log_debug("FPS set from command line: %d", opts->fps);
950     }
951   }
952 
953   // Automatic update check in background thread (once per week maximum)
954   if (!GET_OPTION(no_check_update)) {
955     update_banner_start_check();
956   }
957 
958   // Set up global signal handlers BEFORE mode dispatch
959   // All modes use the same centralized exit mechanism
960   setup_signal_handlers();
961 
962   // Register application callbacks so lib code can check exit flags
963   // This connects the render loop's should_exit check to signal_exit()
964   static const app_callbacks_t app_callbacks = {
965       .should_exit = should_exit,
966       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
967       .platform_get_monotonic_time_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us,
968       .platform_sleep_ms = platform_sleep_ms,
969   };
970   app_callbacks_register(&app_callbacks);
971 
972   // Register shutdown callback so splash thread and other code can check for exit
973   shutdown_register_callback((shutdown_check_fn)should_exit);
974 
975 #ifndef NDEBUG
976   // Start debug threads now, after initialization but before mode entry
977   // This avoids lock contention during critical initialization phase
978   // Note: debug_sync_start_thread() and debug_memory_thread_start() now use
979   // direct pthread calls instead of mutex_init/cond_init to avoid named registry
980   // deadlock during thread startup
981   if (debug_sync_start_thread() != 0) {
982     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Debug sync thread startup failed");
983     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Debug sync thread startup failed");
984   }
985   log_debug("Debug sync thread started");
986 
987 #endif
988 
989   // Find and dispatch to mode entry point
990   const mode_descriptor_t *mode = find_mode(opts->detected_mode);
991   if (!mode) {
992     log_error("Error: Mode not found for detected_mode=%d", opts->detected_mode);
993     return 1;
994   }
995 
996   // Call the mode-specific entry point
997   // Mode entry points use options_get() to access parsed options
998   int exit_code = mode->entry_point();
999 
1000   // Named registry cleanup is handled by asciichat_shared_destroy()
1001   // (called via atexit handler), which runs AFTER the memory report
1002 
1003   if (exit_code == ERROR_USAGE) {
1004     exit(ERROR_USAGE);
1005   }
1006 
1007   return exit_code;
1008 }






References action_show_version(),
    app_callbacks_register(), apply_color_mode_override(),
    apply_palette_config(), ASCII_CHAT_BUILD_TYPE,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_pcre2_cleanup_all(),
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), asciichat_shared_init(),
    asciichat_thread_self, backtrace_print_simple(),
    colorscheme_load_builtin(),
    asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    debug_atomic_init(), debug_memory_set_quiet_mode(),
    debug_sync_init(), debug_sync_set_main_thread_id(),
    debug_sync_start_thread(),
    options_state::debug_sync_state_time,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_state::detected_mode,
    mode_descriptor_t::entry_point, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_PLATFORM_INIT, ERROR_THREAD, ERROR_USAGE,
    FATAL, FILE_PERM_PRIVATE, options_state::fps,
    g_argc, g_argv, g_color_flag_passed,
    g_color_flag_value, GET_OPTION, grep_init(),
    HAS_ERRNO, options_state::help, IS_OPTION_EXPLICIT,
    log_debug, log_dev, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_file, options_state::log_file, log_info,
    log_init_colors(), log_redetect_terminal_capabilities(),
    log_set_color_scheme(), log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_format(), log_set_json_output(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_truncate_if_large(),
    log_warn, MODE_CLIENT, MODE_DISCOVERY,
    MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, MODE_INVALID, MODE_MIRROR,
    MODE_SERVER, named_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_THREAD,
    named_registry_register_packet_types(),
    options_cleanup_schema(), options_colorscheme_init_early(),
    options_get(), options_init(), options_state_destroy(),
    options_state::palette_custom,
    options_state::palette_custom_set,
    path_cleanup_thread_locals(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_open(),
    platform_signal(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    REGISTER_GLOBAL_ATOMICS, SAFE_STRCPY, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    SET_ERRNO, setup_signal_handlers(), should_exit(),
    shutdown_register_callback(), signal_exit(),
    options_state::status_screen, terminal_cursor_show(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    update_banner_start_check(), and options_state::version.






void
  set_interrupt_callback (void(* cb )(void))


Register a mode-specific interrupt callback




Called by mode-specific code to register a callback that will be
    invoked when signal_exit() is called. Used to shut down network
    sockets so threads blocked in recv() unblock quickly instead of waiting for
    timeouts.




The callback is called synchronously from signal_exit(), so
    it must be async-signal-safe (only atomics, socket_shutdown(), no
    malloc/etc).




Only one callback can be registered at a time. Setting a new
    callback replaces the previous one.




Parameters


cb Function to call on exit signal, or NULL to
  unregister


Definition at line 227 of file main.c.


227                                               {
228   g_interrupt_callback = cb;
229 }










void
  setup_signal_handlers (void )


Set up global signal handlers Called once at startup before mode
    dispatch


Definition at line 346 of file main.c.


346                                  {
347   platform_set_console_ctrl_handler(console_ctrl_handler);
348 
349 #ifndef _WIN32
350   platform_signal_handler_t handlers[] = {
351       {SIGINT, handle_sigterm},
352       {SIGTERM, handle_sigterm},
353       {SIGPIPE, SIG_IGN},
354   };
355   platform_register_signal_handlers(handlers, 3);
356   log_debug("Signal handlers registered");
357 #endif
358 }






References log_debug,
    platform_register_signal_handlers(), and
    platform_set_console_ctrl_handler().


Referenced by main().






bool should_exit (void )


Check if the application should exit




Called by mode main loops to detect shutdown requests from signals
    or errors.




Returns


true if shutdown has been requested, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 211 of file main.c.


211                        {
212   return atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit);
213 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.






void
  signal_exit (void )


Signal that the application should exit




Sets the global exit flag and calls the registered interrupt
    callback if set. Safe to call from signal handlers (uses only atomics and
    function pointers). Called by signal handlers (SIGTERM, Ctrl+C) and normal
    code (errors, timeouts).


Definition at line 215 of file main.c.


215                        {
216   // Note: This function may be called from a signal handler context where
217   // other threads may hold mutex locks. We avoid log_console() here to prevent
218   // deadlock (render loop holds terminal lock, signal handler can't acquire it).
219   // The shutdown will be logged by normal thread context later.
220   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
221   void (*cb)(void) = g_interrupt_callback;
222   if (cb) {
223     cb();
224   }
225 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  g_should_exit.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.11.2
    ascii-chat
